message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (for a code change the named test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if reverted; for a doc/decision item, judge whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix H-001 in src/assay_engine/methodology/{hypothesis,confirm}.py + preregistration.py
What: alpha & stability_threshold are optional digest-bound Hypothesis fields; confirmers cross-check confirm-time args against locked values (mismatch raises) — closes threshold HARKing.
Test(s): test_locked_alpha_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time, test_locked_stability_threshold_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time, test_digest_covers_content_and_id_but_not_lock_fields
Read the actual changed file(s); read the test; check no regression. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN. Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify fix H-001 by reading the changed files and tests.

011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
a9f3a48 fix(hardening-p5): batch A — methodology integrity / pre-registration firewall (H-001,002,003,015,016)
b483694 docs(hardening): pass-4 merged (#162); open pass-5 with retrospective dimensions
af29d19 Merge pull request #162 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-4
1a61b89 docs(hardening): record pass-4 (23/24 confirmed, all fixed) + pass-5 dimensions
---
__init__.py
__pycache__
adjudication.py
confirm.py
discovery.py
fence.py
firewalls.py
hypothesis.py
preregistration.py
verdict.py

1	"""Typed, falsifiable hypotheses (METHODOLOGY.md §3).
2	
3	Every hypothesis names what is claimed, the test, the data it runs on, and a pre-specified
4	decision rule. Two origins:
5	
6	- ``DISCOVERY`` — surfaced by the data, then made specific, locked, and timestamped before
7	  confirmation (Firewall B).
8	- ``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` — derived by the adapter from an external expert claim, tested against
9	  the blind baseline (Firewall A).
10	
11	Two kinds, which select the confirmation mechanism:
12	
13	- ``UNIT_LEVEL`` — confirmed on a held-out split.
14	- ``WHOLE_CORPUS`` — no held-out object possible; confirmed against null/permutation
15	  distributions and stability across resamples.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21	from enum import Enum
22	from typing import Any, Literal, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
25	
26	# The tail a claim predicts the statistic lies in, relative to the null.
27	Direction = Literal["greater", "less"]
28	
29	
30	class HypothesisKind(Enum):
31	    UNIT_LEVEL = "unit_level"
32	    WHOLE_CORPUS = "whole_corpus"
33	
34	
35	class HypothesisOrigin(Enum):
36	    DISCOVERY = "discovery"
37	    EXTERNAL_CLAIM = "external_claim"
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
41	class Hypothesis:
42	    """A locked, falsifiable assertion ready for confirmatory testing.
43	
44	    ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` are populated when the hypothesis is sealed for
45	    pre-registration. An unlocked hypothesis (either field ``None``) must not be passed to a
46	    confirmatory test.
47	
48	    Scope of ``locked`` (audit pass 1, issue #6): ``locked`` is only a cheap **presence**
49	    predicate — both fields populated. It is NOT the methodology-grade check and carries no
50	    cryptographic meaning on its own. The real pre-registration verification —
51	    content-binding (the proof covers this hypothesis's content), timestamp attestation, and
52	    lock-before-confirm ordering — lives in
53	    :mod:`assay_engine.methodology.preregistration` (``verify_preregistration`` /
54	    ``require_preregistered``), and the confirmatory runners (:mod:`adjudication`,
55	    :mod:`discovery`) enforce it via a supplied ``TimestampAuthority``. Use
56	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.lock_hypothesis` to produce a hypothesis
57	    whose lock actually verifies; a hand-set ``timestamp_proof`` string satisfies ``locked``
58	    but will fail ``verify_preregistration``.
59	    """
60	
61	    hypothesis_id: str
62	    statement: str
63	    kind: HypothesisKind
64	    origin: HypothesisOrigin
65	    test_name: str
66	    decision_rule: str
67	    source_claim_id: str | None = None
68	    locked_at: str | None = None
69	    timestamp_proof: str | None = None
70	    # The predicted direction, fixed at pre-registration so it cannot be chosen post-hoc
71	    # after seeing the baseline (audit pass 2, issue #24). For an EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypothesis
72	    # the adapter derives this from the claim's assertion, not from the baseline.
73	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None
74	    # Decision thresholds, optionally pre-registered so they cannot be chosen post-hoc after
75	    # seeing the null/baseline (pass 5, #H-001 — the threshold analogue of predicted_direction).
76	    # When set, they are bound into the pre-registration digest and the confirmers cross-check the
77	    # confirm-time argument against them (a mismatch raises), so a pre-registered confirmatory test
78	    # commits its alpha / stability bar in advance. Left None for exploratory/opted-out use.
79	    alpha: float | None = None
80	    stability_threshold: float | None = None
81	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
82	
83	    @property
84	    def locked(self) -> bool:
85	        return self.locked_at is not None and self.timestamp_proof is not None
86	
87	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
88	        if self.origin is HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM and not self.source_claim_id:
89	            raise ValueError("EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypotheses must carry a source_claim_id")
90	        # Validate pre-registered thresholds at construction (#H-001) so an invalid value can never
91	        # be bound into a proof: alpha is a one-sided level in (0, 0.5); stability is a fraction in
92	        # (0, 1]. (Mirrors the confirmers' own bounds checks.)
93	        if self.alpha is not None and not (0.0 < self.alpha < 0.5):
94	            raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in (0, 0.5); got {self.alpha}")
95	        if self.stability_threshold is not None and not (0.0 < self.stability_threshold <= 1.0):
96	            raise ValueError(
97	                f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {self.stability_threshold}"
98	            )
99	        # Validate predicted_direction at the earliest possible point (pass 3, #F-002): an
100	        # invalid tail ("up", "UP", "") must never reach lock_hypothesis (which would
101	        # cryptographically bind garbage into the proof) nor confirm_unit_level (which does not
102	        # otherwise re-validate it). Only the two recognized tails — or None (deferred to
103	        # confirm time on the whole-corpus path) — are admissible.
104	        if self.predicted_direction is not None and self.predicted_direction not in (
105	            "greater",
106	            "less",
107	        ):
108	            raise ValueError(
109	                "predicted_direction must be 'greater' or 'less' (or None); got "
110	                f"{self.predicted_direction!r} — an unrecognized tail would silently flip the "
111	                "supported/contradicted verdict"
112	            )
113	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
114

1	"""Pre-registration integrity (METHODOLOGY.md §3, GOVERNANCE.md §2).
2	
3	GOVERNANCE §2 defines pre-registration as three steps: a hypothesis is (1) made specific and
4	typed, (2) **content-hashed and committed**, and (3) **timestamped against a trusted authority**
5	— *before* any confirmatory test. Steps 2–3 were a presence sentinel until now: ``Hypothesis.locked``
6	asserted only that ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` were non-``None``, so a hand-written
7	``timestamp_proof="rfc3161:demo"`` "locked" a hypothesis and nothing stopped a study from
8	locking *after* it had seen the baseline, or from swapping the hypothesis content after locking
9	(audit pass 1, issue #6). Both firewalls (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) and all of
10	:mod:`confirm` rest on the lock, so a sentinel lock made the three verdicts decorative.
11	
12	This module makes the lock mean what the method says, by construction:
13	
14	- **Content binding** — :func:`canonical_hypothesis_digest` is a type-faithful SHA-256 over a
15	  hypothesis's *decision-bearing* fields (statement, kind, origin, test, decision rule,
16	  predicted direction, source claim, params). A valid proof must attest to *this* digest, so
17	  the content the verdict is about is provably the content that was locked: change the claim,
18	  the test, or the decision rule after locking and verification fails.
19	- **Verifiable timestamp** — a pluggable :class:`TimestampAuthority` returns a
20	  :class:`VerifiedTimestamp` only if the proof genuinely binds the digest. The engine ships one
21	  real, data-sovereign authority (:class:`LocalHmacAuthority`, on-box HMAC) and the *Protocol*
22	  so a study can plug an RFC-3161 TSA. It ships **no** silent-accept authority.
23	- **Lock before confirm** — :func:`require_preregistered` rejects a lock whose attested time is
24	  not strictly before the confirmation moment, so a hypothesis cannot be "pre"-registered after
25	  the result is known.
26	
27	Honest scope (the lesson carried from ADR-0008): :class:`LocalHmacAuthority` proves content
28	integrity, time binding, and unforgeability *relative to a secret that lives on this box*.
29	Whoever holds that secret could backdate a proof — it is local tamper-evidence, **not**
30	third-party non-repudiation. A study that needs non-repudiation wires an RFC-3161 authority
31	(which sends only the digest, never the data, so it stays compatible with data sovereignty —
32	ADR-0003 — at the cost of an external dependency the study opts into). And content binding
33	covers the *structured fields*: free text inside ``statement``/``params`` is hashed (so it
34	cannot change after locking) but the engine cannot judge whether that text is *meaningful* —
35	that remains a review obligation (GOVERNANCE §2 "timestamped adaptive design").
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import datetime as _dt
41	import hmac
42	from dataclasses import dataclass, replace
43	from typing import Protocol
44	
45	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json, hash_text
46	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import FirewallViolation
47	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis
48	
49	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
50	_PROOF_PREFIX = "local-hmac:v1:"
51	_PROOF_FIELD_SEP = "|"  # ISO-8601 and lowercase hex never contain "|"
52	
53	
54	class PreRegistrationError(FirewallViolation):
55	    """A hypothesis failed pre-registration verification (METHODOLOGY §3, GOVERNANCE §2).
56	
57	    A subclass of :class:`FirewallViolation` so existing ``except FirewallViolation`` handlers
58	    in the runners and studies treat a bad lock as the firewall breach it is.
59	    """
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
63	class VerifiedTimestamp:
64	    """The authority's attestation that ``digest`` existed at ``instant``."""
65	
66	    instant: _dt.datetime  # timezone-aware, normalized to UTC
67	    authority: str
68	    digest: str
69	
70	
71	class TimestampAuthority(Protocol):
72	    """Engine contract: verify that ``proof`` binds ``digest``, returning the attested time.
73	
74	    Implementations MUST raise :class:`PreRegistrationError` (not return a sentinel) when the
75	    proof is malformed, does not bind ``digest``, or is otherwise invalid — the engine treats a
76	    non-raising return as a genuine attestation.
77	    """
78	
79	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp: ...
80	
81	
82	class StampingAuthority(TimestampAuthority, Protocol):
83	    """A study-side authority that can also *issue* proofs (used by :func:`lock_hypothesis`)."""
84	
85	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str: ...
86	
87	
88	def _require_aware_utc(instant: _dt.datetime, what: str) -> _dt.datetime:
89	    if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
90	        raise PreRegistrationError(
91	            f"{what} must be timezone-aware (a naive datetime has no defined instant)"
92	        )
93	    return instant.astimezone(_UTC)
94	
95	
96	def canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> str:
97	    """A type-faithful SHA-256 over a hypothesis's decision-bearing content **and its id**.
98	
99	    Includes ``hypothesis_id``: although it is a label, it is fixed at lock time and is the key
100	    the runners attribute verdicts by (``verdict.hypothesis_id``), so leaving it unbound would
101	    let a post-lock id swap file a verdict under an id the proof never attested (adversarial
102	    review, #80). Excludes only the lock fields themselves (``locked_at``/``timestamp_proof`` —
103	    they attest *to* this digest, so binding them would be circular).
104	
105	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the content cannot be canonicalized reproducibly
106	    (a non-finite float or an unhashable leaf in ``params``) — the pre-registration entrypoints
107	    must surface a typed firewall error, not a bare ``ValueError``/``TypeError`` (#82).
108	    """
109	    payload = {
110	        "hypothesis_id": hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
111	        "statement": hypothesis.statement,
112	        "kind": hypothesis.kind.value,
113	        "origin": hypothesis.origin.value,
114	        "test_name": hypothesis.test_name,
115	        "decision_rule": hypothesis.decision_rule,
116	        "source_claim_id": hypothesis.source_claim_id,
117	        "predicted_direction": hypothesis.predicted_direction,
118	        # decision thresholds are decision-bearing content — bind them so a pre-registered alpha /
119	        # stability bar cannot be changed post-hoc (pass 5, #H-001).
120	        "alpha": hypothesis.alpha,
121	        "stability_threshold": hypothesis.stability_threshold,
122	        "params": dict(hypothesis.params),
123	    }
124	    try:
125	        return hash_text(canonical_json(payload))
126	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
127	        raise PreRegistrationError(
128	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} cannot be canonicalized for a content "
129	            f"digest ({exc}); pre-registration requires reproducibly hashable content "
130	            "(no non-finite floats or unhashable leaves in params)"
131	        ) from None
132	
133	
134	class LocalHmacAuthority:
135	    """A real, on-box timestamp authority binding a digest and an instant under a secret key.
136	
137	    ``stamp`` issues ``local-hmac:v1:<instant-iso>|<hmac-hex>`` where the MAC is
138	    ``HMAC-SHA256(secret, digest || instant)``. ``verify`` recomputes the MAC over the digest
139	    the engine derived from the *current* hypothesis and the instant carried in the proof, and
140	    constant-time compares. The proof therefore verifies iff the content is unchanged, the
141	    instant is unchanged, and the same secret is used — without the secret it cannot be forged
142	    for arbitrary content or time.
143	
144	    Honest scope: this is local tamper-evidence, not third-party non-repudiation — the holder of
145	    ``secret`` could backdate (see module docstring). Use an RFC-3161 authority when you need a
146	    third party to vouch for the time.
147	    """
148	
149	    authority_id = "local-hmac"
150	
151	    def __init__(self, secret: bytes) -> None:
152	        if not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16:
153	            raise ValueError("LocalHmacAuthority secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
154	        self._secret = bytes(secret)
155	
156	    def _mac(self, digest: str, instant_iso: str) -> str:
157	        msg = canonical_json(["local-hmac:v1", digest, instant_iso]).encode("utf-8")
158	        return hmac.new(self._secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
159	
160	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str:
161	        when = instant if instant is not None else _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
162	        when = _require_aware_utc(when, "stamp instant")
163	        instant_iso = when.isoformat()
164	        return f"{_PROOF_PREFIX}{instant_iso}{_PROOF_FIELD_SEP}{self._mac(digest, instant_iso)}"
165	
166	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
167	        if not proof.startswith(_PROOF_PREFIX):
168	            raise PreRegistrationError(
169	                f"proof is not a {self.authority_id} v1 token (wrong prefix)"
170	            )
171	        body = proof[len(_PROOF_PREFIX) :]
172	        instant_iso, sep, mac_hex = body.partition(_PROOF_FIELD_SEP)
173	        if not sep or not instant_iso or not mac_hex:
174	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (expected <instant>|<mac>)")
175	        # The MAC field must be exactly the hex of a SHA-256 digest. Validating its shape here
176	        # (a) rejects garbage early and (b) keeps non-ASCII/non-hex out of hmac.compare_digest,
177	        # which raises TypeError on non-ASCII str rather than returning False (#81).
178	        if len(mac_hex) != 64 or any(c not in "0123456789abcdef" for c in mac_hex):
179	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (mac is not 64 lowercase hex)")
180	        try:
181	            parsed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(instant_iso)
182	        except ValueError:
183	            raise PreRegistrationError(f"proof instant is not ISO-8601: {instant_iso!r}") from None
184	        instant = _require_aware_utc(parsed, "proof instant")
185	        expected = self._mac(digest, instant_iso)
186	        if not hmac.compare_digest(expected, mac_hex):
187	            # Either the content (digest) changed since locking, the instant was tampered, or the
188	            # proof was issued under a different secret. All are pre-registration failures.
189	            raise PreRegistrationError(
190	                "proof does not bind this hypothesis's content at the stated time "
191	                "(content changed after locking, or proof was forged/issued by another authority)"
192	            )
193	        return VerifiedTimestamp(instant=instant, authority=self.authority_id, digest=digest)
194	
195	
196	def lock_hypothesis(
197	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
198	    *,
199	    authority: StampingAuthority,
200	    instant: _dt.datetime | None = None,
201	) -> Hypothesis:
202	    """Return a locked copy of ``hypothesis`` whose proof binds its current content.
203	
204	    The engine's blessed way to pre-register: it computes the content digest, has ``authority``
205	    stamp it, and records the attested instant in ``locked_at`` so the displayed lock time and
206	    the cryptographic proof always agree. Re-locking an already-locked hypothesis is refused.
207	
208	    Scope of the seal (same honest boundary as ADR-0008): this refuses re-locking *this object*.
209	    It cannot stop a study that willfully reconstructs a hypothesis — e.g. ``dataclasses.replace``
210	    to clear the lock fields, then re-lock different content — any more than the runners can stop
211	    a builder that reflects into their frame; both need process isolation, which is out of scope.
212	    What the engine *does* guarantee is that whatever is ultimately confirmed has a proof binding
213	    its content at an attested time before confirmation. So a re-locked copy is simply a new
214	    pre-registration with a new, later timestamp — visible as such, not a silent override.
215	    """
216	    if hypothesis.locked:
217	        raise PreRegistrationError(
218	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is already locked; re-locking is forbidden"
219	        )
220	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
221	    proof = authority.stamp(digest, instant=instant)
222	    verified = authority.verify(digest, proof)  # fail loud here if the authority is misbehaving
223	    return replace(hypothesis, locked_at=verified.instant.isoformat(), timestamp_proof=proof)
224	
225	
226	def verify_preregistration(
227	    hypothesis: Hypothesis, *, authority: TimestampAuthority
228	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
229	    """Verify a hypothesis is genuinely pre-registered: present, content-bound, time-attested.
230	
231	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the hypothesis is not locked, the proof does not
232	    bind the *current* content digest, the attested instant is not a timezone-aware time, or the
233	    self-reported ``locked_at`` disagrees with the authority's attested instant.
234	    """
235	    if not hypothesis.locked:
236	        raise PreRegistrationError(
237	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
238	            "before pre-registration (METHODOLOGY §3 / GOVERNANCE §2)"
239	        )
240	    assert hypothesis.timestamp_proof is not None  # .locked guarantees this; for the type checker
241	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
242	    verified = authority.verify(digest, hypothesis.timestamp_proof)
243	    if verified.digest != digest:
244	        raise PreRegistrationError(
245	            f"authority attested digest {verified.digest!r} but the hypothesis content hashes to "
246	            f"{digest!r} — the proof does not cover this hypothesis"
247	        )
248	    # The displayed lock time must match the attested one, so a study cannot show a time the
249	    # authority did not vouch for.
250	    try:
251	        claimed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(hypothesis.locked_at or "")
252	    except ValueError:
253	        raise PreRegistrationError(
254	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} is not an ISO-8601 timestamp"
255	        ) from None
256	    if _require_aware_utc(claimed, "locked_at") != verified.instant:
257	        raise PreRegistrationError(
258	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} disagrees with the authority-attested instant "
259	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}"
260	        )
261	    return verified
262	
263	
264	def require_preregistered(
265	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
266	    *,
267	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
268	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
269	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
270	    """Gate a confirmatory step: the hypothesis must be verifiably locked *before* ``not_after``.
271	
272	    ``not_after`` is the confirmation moment (a runner captures it at entry). A lock whose
273	    attested instant is not strictly earlier is rejected — you cannot pre-register a hypothesis
274	    after the result that would confirm it is already in hand.
275	    """
276	    not_after = _require_aware_utc(not_after, "not_after")
277	    verified = verify_preregistration(hypothesis, authority=authority)
278	    if not verified.instant < not_after:
279	        raise PreRegistrationError(
280	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} was locked at "
281	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}, not strictly before the confirmation moment "
282	            f"{not_after.isoformat()!r} — a lock must precede confirmation (GOVERNANCE §2)"
283	        )
284	    return verified
285

1	"""Confirmatory-test machinery (METHODOLOGY.md §2, §4).
2	
3	A confirmatory test takes a *locked* hypothesis and returns a :class:`Verdict`. Two
4	mechanisms, selected by hypothesis kind:
5	
6	- unit-level → evaluate on a held-out confirmation partition (Firewall B via
7	  :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`);
8	- whole-corpus → compare the observed statistic against a null/permutation distribution and
9	  require stability across resamples.
10	
11	This module defines the *contract and guards* around confirmation. The concrete statistics
12	are supplied by callers via the baseline toolkit; the engine's responsibility here is to
13	refuse to confirm an unlocked hypothesis or on discovery data, validate the supplied
14	statistics, and map an outcome onto one of the three verdicts honestly (including
15	``indeterminate``).
16	
17	Direction handling (audit pass 1, issue #2): a claim predicts the statistic lies in a
18	particular tail of the null. The empirical p-value is computed against *that* tail, so a
19	claim predicting a low value is confirmable, and a genuine contradiction (significant in the
20	opposite tail) is detectable — both were impossible under the previous direction-blind
21	upper-tail-only implementation.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import datetime as _dt
27	import math
28	import warnings
29	from typing import Iterable, Sequence, cast
30	
31	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
32	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Direction, Hypothesis, HypothesisKind
33	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
34	    TimestampAuthority,
35	    require_preregistered,
36	)
37	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
38	
39	
40	def _gate_preregistration(hypothesis: Hypothesis, authority: TimestampAuthority | None) -> None:
41	    """Pre-registration gate for the confirm primitives.
42	
43	    If ``authority`` is supplied, run the full :func:`require_preregistered` check (content
44	    binding + verifiable timestamp + lock-before-this-moment) — so a study calling a confirm
45	    primitive *directly* (outside a runner) gets the same guarantee the runners enforce. If it
46	    is ``None``, fall back to the cheap presence gate (:func:`require_locked`); the caller has
47	    then opted out of real verification and owns that choice.
48	    """
49	    if authority is not None:
50	        require_preregistered(
51	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
52	        )
53	    else:
54	        # Falling back to the cheap presence gate means a hand-set timestamp_proof string passes
55	        # WITHOUT any cryptographic verification. Warn so a direct caller cannot silently skip real
56	        # pre-registration (#G-006); the runners always supply an authority and never hit this.
57	        warnings.warn(
58	            "confirm primitive called with authority=None: pre-registration is verified by a "
59	            "PRESENCE check only (lock fields populated), NOT cryptographically. Pass a "
60	            "TimestampAuthority to verify the proof binds the hypothesis and precedes confirmation.",
61	            stacklevel=3,
62	        )
63	        require_locked(hypothesis)
64	
65	
66	def require_locked(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> None:
67	    """Cheap **presence** gate: refuse a hypothesis that carries no lock fields at all.
68	
69	    This is defense-in-depth, not the methodology-grade check. It does not verify the proof
70	    binds the content or that the lock precedes confirmation — that is
71	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered`, which the
72	    confirmatory *runners* (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) call before they confirm.
73	    A study driving :func:`confirm_whole_corpus`/:func:`confirm_unit_level` directly (outside a
74	    runner) should call ``require_preregistered`` itself to get the real guarantee.
75	    """
76	    if not hypothesis.locked:
77	        raise FirewallViolation(
78	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
79	            "before pre-registration (Firewall B / pre-registration)"
80	        )
81	
82	
83	def _validate_alpha(alpha: float) -> None:
84	    # A significance threshold of 0.5 or more is not a meaningful one-sided level, and
85	    # admitting it lets both tails register "significant" at once (alpha < 0.5 guarantees
86	    # p_support + p_contra >= 1 cannot both fall below alpha) — fix-review nit on issue #2.
87	    if not (0.0 < alpha < 0.5):
88	        raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in (0, 0.5); got {alpha}")
89	
90	
91	def _validate_pvalue(p_value: float) -> None:
92	    if not (0.0 <= p_value <= 1.0):
93	        raise ValueError(f"p_value must be in [0, 1]; got {p_value}")
94	
95	
96	def _validate_finite(name: str, value: float) -> None:
97	    if not math.isfinite(value):
98	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a finite number; got {value}")
99	
100	
101	def verdict_from_pvalue(
102	    hypothesis_id: str,
103	    *,
104	    statistic: float,
105	    p_value: float,
106	    alpha: float,
107	    powered: bool,
108	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
109	    test_description: str = "confirmatory test",
110	) -> Verdict:
111	    """Map a confirmatory statistic onto the three verdicts.
112	
113	    - not ``powered`` → ``indeterminate`` (the method cannot decide).
114	    - significant and direction agrees with the claim → ``supported``.
115	    - significant and direction disagrees → ``contradicted``.
116	    - not significant → ``indeterminate`` (absence of evidence is not contradiction here).
117	
118	    ``p_value`` and ``direction_supports_claim`` must be derived by the caller from a test
119	    appropriate to the claim's predicted direction. For a unit-level held-out test the engine
120	    is handed only an opaque ``statistic`` + ``p_value`` and cannot itself infer whether the
121	    statistic supports or contradicts the claim — so ``direction_supports_claim`` is a trusted
122	    caller obligation, ``statistic`` is recorded for provenance only (it does NOT gate the
123	    verdict), and the deciding flag is recorded in ``evidence`` so the verdict is re-derivable
124	    (audit pass 2, issue #20). Inputs are validated (issue #4) and the recorded decision rule
125	    reflects the caller's ``test_description``, not a hard-coded sidedness label (issue #2).
126	
127	    ``powered`` is likewise a TRUSTED CALLER OBLIGATION (pass 5, #H-002): the engine cannot
128	    compute statistical power from an opaque statistic/p-value, so the caller asserts whether the
129	    test was adequately powered (``powered=False`` forces ``indeterminate``). The engine cannot
130	    verify it but records it in ``evidence`` so the verdict's dependence on the flag is auditable.
131	    """
132	    _validate_pvalue(p_value)
133	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
134	    _validate_finite("statistic", statistic)
135	
136	    rule = f"{test_description} at alpha={alpha}; powered={powered}"
137	    common = dict(
138	        statistic=statistic,
139	        threshold=alpha,
140	        evidence={
141	            "p_value": p_value,
142	            "direction_supports_claim": direction_supports_claim,
143	            "powered": powered,  # trusted caller flag, recorded for audit (#H-002)
144	        },
145	    )
146	    if not powered:
147	        return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="underpowered", **common)
148	    if p_value <= alpha:
149	        if direction_supports_claim:
150	            return Verdict.supported(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
151	        return Verdict.contradicted(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
152	    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="not significant at alpha", **common)
153	
154	
155	def confirm_unit_level(
156	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
157	    *,
158	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
159	    evaluated_ids: Iterable[str],
160	    statistic: float,
161	    p_value: float,
162	    alpha: float,
163	    powered: bool,
164	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
165	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
166	) -> Verdict:
167	    """Confirm a unit-level hypothesis on the held-out partition only.
168	
169	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
170	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
171	
172	    Direction (pass 4, #G-017): unlike :func:`confirm_whole_corpus` — where the verdict polarity
173	    is derived from the pre-registered ``predicted_direction`` tail — the unit-level verdict is
174	    governed SOLELY by the caller-supplied ``direction_supports_claim`` flag (the engine sees only
175	    an opaque held-out ``statistic``/``p_value`` and cannot itself infer the tail). The
176	    hypothesis's ``predicted_direction`` is therefore advisory at unit level: it is shape-validated
177	    below for safety but does NOT decide the verdict. A study that wants the locked tail to bind the
178	    unit-level decision must derive ``direction_supports_claim`` from it in its own confirmer.
179	
180	    Consequently (pass 5, #H-015) the unit-level path deliberately does NOT mirror
181	    :func:`confirm_whole_corpus`'s directionless-lock REFUSAL: because the locked direction never
182	    drives the unit-level verdict, a directionless lock here is harmless, not a HARKing vector —
183	    the asymmetry is intentional, not a missing guard.
184	
185	    Test identity (pass 5, #H-003): ``test_name`` is bound into the pre-registration proof for
186	    audit, but the engine receives only an opaque ``statistic``/``p_value`` and CANNOT verify that
187	    the test actually executed matches the locked ``test_name`` — that remains a caller obligation.
188	    """
189	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL:
190	        raise ValueError("confirm_unit_level requires a UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis")
191	    # Defense-in-depth direction validation (pass 3, #F-002): the unit-level path takes the
192	    # deciding direction as a trusted caller flag (``direction_supports_claim``) and so never
193	    # routed through ``_resolve_direction`` — which left a locked-but-invalid
194	    # ``predicted_direction`` (e.g. "UP") entirely unchecked here. ``Hypothesis.__post_init__``
195	    # now rejects such values at construction, but re-validate any pre-registered direction here
196	    # so a hypothesis constructed by an unusual path (or a future field mutation) cannot confirm
197	    # against an unrecognized tail.
198	    if hypothesis.predicted_direction is not None and (
199	        hypothesis.predicted_direction not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS
200	    ):
201	        raise ValueError(
202	            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got "
203	            f"{hypothesis.predicted_direction!r}"
204	        )
205	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
206	    split.assert_confirm_only(evaluated_ids)  # Firewall B (rejects empty / discovery ids)
207	    alpha = cast(float, _resolve_locked_param(hypothesis.alpha, alpha, "alpha"))  # #H-001
208	    return verdict_from_pvalue(
209	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
210	        statistic=statistic,
211	        p_value=p_value,
212	        alpha=alpha,
213	        powered=powered,
214	        direction_supports_claim=direction_supports_claim,
215	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
216	    )
217	
218	
219	def _empirical_p(null: Sequence[float], observed: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
220	    """One-sided empirical p-value with +1 smoothing for the given tail."""
221	    n = len(null)
222	    if tail == "greater":
223	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v >= observed)
224	    else:
225	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v <= observed)
226	    return (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
227	
228	
229	def _resample_stability(
230	    resamples: Sequence[float], null: Sequence[float], tail: Direction, alpha: float
231	) -> float:
232	    """Fraction of resamples that **reproduce the observed effect**.
233	
234	    Each resample is scored as its own observed statistic against the null and must itself be
235	    significant in the predicted tail (empirical p ≤ ``alpha``). This measures reproduction of
236	    the observed *effect* — not merely landing on the predicted side of the null center, which a
237	    resample distribution clearing the null median by an epsilon would satisfy while reproducing
238	    nothing of the observed magnitude (audit pass 2, #139). The closer to 1.0, the more
239	    consistently the effect's significance reproduces across resamples.
240	
241	    The default path is O(R·N) pure Python (one null pass per resample). When numpy is available
242	    (the optional ``baseline`` extra) the whole R×N comparison runs as a single vectorized op
243	    (pass 3, #F-017). The numpy path computes the IDENTICAL integer extreme-counts and the same
244	    ``(extreme + 1) / (n + 1) <= alpha`` test, so its result is exactly equal to the Python path;
245	    it is purely a speed optimization for large R·N, never a semantic change.
246	    """
247	    n = len(null)
248	    try:
249	        import numpy as np
250	
251	        # Use searchsorted on a sorted null (O((R+N) log N) time, O(R) memory) rather than the
252	        # R×N broadcast boolean matrix (#G-010): the matrix peaks at ~R*N bytes (250MB for
253	        # R=5000,N=50000) and can OOM where the pure-Python loop would not. The integer
254	        # extreme-counts are IDENTICAL to _empirical_p: for 'greater', count(null >= r) = N -
255	        # searchsorted(sorted, r, 'left'); for 'less', count(null <= r) = searchsorted(sorted, r,
256	        # 'right'). Then (extreme+1)/(n+1) <= alpha, same as the pure path.
257	        sorted_null = np.sort(np.asarray(null, dtype=np.float64))
258	        res_arr = np.asarray(resamples, dtype=np.float64)
259	        if tail == "greater":
260	            extreme = n - np.searchsorted(sorted_null, res_arr, side="left")
261	        else:
262	            extreme = np.searchsorted(sorted_null, res_arr, side="right")
263	        p = (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
264	        return float(int((p <= alpha).sum()) / len(resamples))
265	    except ImportError:
266	        significant = sum(1 for r in resamples if _empirical_p(null, r, tail) <= alpha)
267	        return significant / len(resamples)
268	
269	
270	def _resolve_locked_param(locked: float | None, supplied: float | None, name: str) -> float | None:
271	    """Cross-check a confirm-time threshold against the pre-registered one (pass 5, #H-001).
272	
273	    If the hypothesis locked the value, the confirm-time argument may only AGREE with it (a
274	    mismatch raises) — the analyst cannot pick alpha / the stability bar after seeing the data
275	    (threshold HARKing). The locked value wins. If nothing was locked, the supplied value stands
276	    (exploratory / opted-out use), mirroring how direction worked before it could be locked.
277	    """
278	    if locked is not None:
279	        if supplied is not None and supplied != locked:
280	            raise ValueError(
281	                f"{name} argument {supplied!r} contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
282	                f"{name} {locked!r} — the threshold is fixed at lock time (no post-hoc choice)"
283	            )
284	        return locked
285	    return supplied
286	
287	
288	_VALID_DIRECTIONS = ("greater", "less")
289	
290	
291	def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
292	    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time argument is
293	    optional and, if given, must MATCH the pre-registered one — it can cross-check, never supply.
294	
295	    A hypothesis reaching here has already passed the confirm gate, so it is locked and its
296	    ``predicted_direction`` is part of the cryptographic binding. A ``None`` locked direction
297	    therefore means the tail was *not* pre-registered; accepting a confirm-time direction in that
298	    case would let the analyst pick the tail AFTER seeing the data (HARKing), flipping
299	    supported↔contradicted on a hypothesis whose lock attests "no direction" (audit pass 4,
300	    #G-001). The tail must be committed before confirmation, so a directionless locked hypothesis
301	    is refused outright rather than completed with a post-hoc direction.
302	
303	    The resolved direction is validated against ``{"greater", "less"}``; any other value is
304	    rejected (an unrecognized tail would silently fall through to 'less' in :func:`_empirical_p`,
305	    flipping the verdict — audit pass 2, #128).
306	    """
307	    locked_dir = hypothesis.predicted_direction
308	    if locked_dir is None:
309	        raise ValueError(
310	            "predicted_direction was not pre-registered: a whole-corpus confirmatory hypothesis "
311	            "must lock its tail ('greater' or 'less') BEFORE confirmation. Supplying a confirm-time "
312	            "direction for a directionless locked hypothesis selects the tail after seeing the data "
313	            "(HARKing) and is refused (#G-001)."
314	        )
315	    if predicted_direction is not None and predicted_direction != locked_dir:
316	        raise ValueError(
317	            "predicted_direction argument contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
318	            f"direction ({predicted_direction!r} != {locked_dir!r})"
319	        )
320	    resolved = locked_dir
321	    if resolved not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS:
322	        raise ValueError(
323	            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got {resolved!r} — an "
324	            "unrecognized tail would silently flip the supported/contradicted verdict"
325	        )
326	    return resolved
327	
328	
329	def confirm_whole_corpus(
330	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
331	    *,
332	    observed: float,
333	    null_distribution: Sequence[float],
334	    alpha: float,
335	    resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
336	    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
337	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
338	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
339	) -> Verdict:
340	    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.
341	
342	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
343	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
344	
345	    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
346	    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
347	    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
348	    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:
349	
350	    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
351	    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
352	    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.
353	
354	    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
355	    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
356	    """
357	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
358	        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
359	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
360	    # Cross-check the confirm-time thresholds against any pre-registered ones (#H-001): a locked
361	    # alpha / stability bar cannot be overridden post-hoc.
362	    alpha = cast(float, _resolve_locked_param(hypothesis.alpha, alpha, "alpha"))
363	    stability_threshold = cast(
364	        float,
365	        _resolve_locked_param(
366	            hypothesis.stability_threshold, stability_threshold, "stability_threshold"
367	        ),
368	    )
369	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
370	    _validate_finite("observed", observed)
371	    if not null_distribution:
372	        raise ValueError(
373	            "a null/permutation distribution is required for whole-corpus confirmation"
374	        )
375	    for v in null_distribution:
376	        _validate_finite("null_distribution value", v)
377	    if not (0.0 < stability_threshold <= 1.0):
378	        raise ValueError(f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {stability_threshold}")
379	
380	    direction = _resolve_direction(hypothesis, predicted_direction)
381	    opposite: Direction = "less" if direction == "greater" else "greater"
382	    p_support = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, direction)
383	    p_contra = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, opposite)
384	    n = len(null_distribution)
385	
386	    stability: float | None = None
387	    contra_stability: float | None = None
388	    if resample_statistics is not None:
389	        if not resample_statistics:
390	            raise ValueError("resample_statistics, if given, must be non-empty")
391	        for r in resample_statistics:
392	            _validate_finite("resample statistic", r)
393	        stability = _resample_stability(resample_statistics, null_distribution, direction, alpha)
394	        # Also measure how consistently the resamples reproduce the OPPOSITE-tail significance, so a
395	        # contradiction's robustness is recorded in evidence even though (by design) a contradiction
396	        # is reportable without it (#G-005).
397	        contra_stability = _resample_stability(
398	            resample_statistics, null_distribution, opposite, alpha
399	        )
400	    stable = stability is not None and stability >= stability_threshold
401	
402	    rule = (
403	        f"empirical p from {n}-sample null at alpha={alpha}, predicted tail '{direction}'; "
404	        f"stability across resamples >= {stability_threshold} (engine-measured)"
405	    )
406	    evidence = {
407	        "p_support": p_support,
408	        "p_contradict": p_contra,
409	        "null_n": n,
410	        "predicted_direction": direction,
411	        "stability": stability,
412	        "contradiction_stability": contra_stability,
413	        "stability_threshold": stability_threshold,
414	        "n_resamples": len(resample_statistics) if resample_statistics is not None else 0,
415	    }
416	    common = dict(statistic=observed, threshold=alpha, evidence=evidence)
417	
418	    # A contradiction (significant in the OPPOSITE tail) is reportable without a stability gate, and
419	    # this asymmetry with `supported` is INTENTIONAL (#G-005): support is a positive claim that must
420	    # reproduce to be decisive, whereas a statistic landing significantly in the wrong tail is
421	    # evidence OF the opposite — not absence of evidence — so it stands on the observed effect.
422	    # `contradiction_stability` is recorded in evidence (when resamples were supplied) so an auditor
423	    # can see whether the contradiction also reproduces, even though it is not gated on it.
424	    if p_contra <= alpha:
425	        return Verdict.contradicted(
426	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="significant in the opposite tail", **common
427	        )
428	    if stability is None:
429	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
430	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
431	            rule,
432	            notes="stability not assessed (no resamples supplied)",
433	            **common,
434	        )
435	    if not stable:
436	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
437	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="unstable across resamples", **common
438	        )
439	    if p_support <= alpha:
440	        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
441	    return Verdict.indeterminate(
442	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="does not beat null at alpha", **common
443	    )
444

content.text: The implementation looks solid. Now let me read the tests to verify they discriminate.

content.content:
230	    # a genuinely reproducing resample distribution (each significant vs the null) is still stable
231	    strong = [100.0] * 100
232	    v2 = confirm_whole_corpus(
233	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="greater"),
234	        observed=100.0,
235	        null_distribution=null,
236	        alpha=0.05,
237	        resample_statistics=strong,
238	        predicted_direction="greater",
239	    )
240	    assert v2.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED and v2.evidence["stability"] == 1.0
241	
242	
243	def test_locked_alpha_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time():
244	    # #H-001: alpha is decision-bearing; if pre-registered on the hypothesis, a confirm-time alpha
245	    # may only AGREE with it. A different alpha (chosen after seeing the data) is refused — closing
246	    # the threshold-HARKing path the direction guard (#G-001) closed for the tail.
247	    h = Hypothesis(
248	        hypothesis_id="h-a",
249	        statement="x",
250	        kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS,
251	        origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
252	        test_name="t",
253	        decision_rule="r",
254	        locked_at="2026-06-15T00:00:00Z",
255	        timestamp_proof="rfc3161:deadbeef",
256	        predicted_direction="greater",
257	        alpha=0.05,
258	    )
259	    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered alpha"):
260	        confirm_whole_corpus(
261	            h,
262	            observed=10.0,
263	            null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
264	            alpha=0.10,
265	            resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
266	        )
267	    # the matching alpha is accepted and the locked value governs
268	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
269	        h, observed=10.0, null_distribution=[0.0] * 100, alpha=0.05, resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI
270	    )
271	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
272	
273	
274	def test_locked_stability_threshold_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time():
275	    # #H-001: the stability bar is decision-bearing too — a pre-registered stability_threshold
276	    # cannot be lowered post-hoc to flip indeterminate->supported.
277	    h = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="greater")
278	    object.__setattr__(h, "stability_threshold", 0.9)  # simulate a locked stability bar
279	    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered stability"):
280	        confirm_whole_corpus(
281	            h,
282	            observed=10.0,
283	            null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
284	            alpha=0.05,
285	            resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
286	            stability_threshold=0.6,
287	        )
288	
289	
290	def test_whole_corpus_records_contradiction_stability_in_evidence():
291	    # #G-005: a contradiction is reportable without a stability gate (intentional asymmetry — it is
292	    # evidence OF the opposite, not absence of evidence), but the opposite-tail stability must be
293	    # RECORDED in evidence (when resamples were supplied) so an auditor sees whether it reproduces.
294	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
295	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="greater"),
296	        observed=0.0,
297	        null_distribution=[10.0] * 100,
298	        alpha=0.05,
299	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_LO,  # reproduce the opposite (low) tail
300	    )
301	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED
302	    assert v.evidence["contradiction_stability"] == 1.0  # surfaced, even though not gated on
303	
304	
305	def test_confirm_primitive_warns_on_presence_only_gate():
306	    # #G-006: calling a confirm primitive with authority=None verifies pre-registration by PRESENCE
307	    # only (no cryptographic check); the insecure fallback must warn, not pass silently.
308	    with pytest.warns(UserWarning, match="authority=None"):
309	        confirm_whole_corpus(
310	            _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="greater"),
311	            observed=10.0,
312	            null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
313	            alpha=0.05,
314	            resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
315	            authority=None,
316	        )
317	
318	
319	def test_unit_level_verdict_is_governed_by_caller_flag_not_locked_direction():
320	    # #G-017: at unit level the verdict polarity is decided by direction_supports_claim, NOT the
321	    # locked predicted_direction (which is advisory). A hypothesis locked 'greater' with the flag
322	    # False yields CONTRADICTED — the locked tail does not override the flag.
323	    v = confirm_unit_level(
324	        _locked(HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL, predicted_direction="greater"),
325	        split=_split(),
326	        evaluated_ids={"c", "d"},
327	        statistic=2.0,
328	        p_value=0.01,
329	        alpha=0.05,
330	        powered=True,
331	        direction_supports_claim=False,
332	    )
333	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED
334	
335	
336	def test_whole_corpus_supported_upper_tail():
337	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
338	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="greater"),
339	        observed=10.0,
340	        null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
341	        alpha=0.05,
342	        resample_statistics=_STABLE_HI,
343	        predicted_direction="greater",
344	    )
345	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED
346	    assert v.evidence["stability"] == 1.0  # engine-measured, not asserted (#21)
347	
348	
349	def test_whole_corpus_supported_lower_tail():

51	def test_digest_covers_content_and_id_but_not_lock_fields():
52	    h = _unlocked()
53	    # hypothesis_id IS bound (#80): it is the verdict's attribution key, so a swap must be caught
54	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(h) != canonical_hypothesis_digest(
55	        replace(h, hypothesis_id="X")
56	    )
57	    # decision-bearing content changes the digest
58	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(h) != canonical_hypothesis_digest(
59	        replace(h, decision_rule="r2")
60	    )
61	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(h) != canonical_hypothesis_digest(replace(h, statement="s2"))
62	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(h) != canonical_hypothesis_digest(
63	        replace(h, predicted_direction="greater")
64	    )
65	    # #H-001: pre-registered decision thresholds are decision-bearing and must be bound, so they
66	    # cannot be changed post-hoc without invalidating the proof.
67	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(h) != canonical_hypothesis_digest(replace(h, alpha=0.05))
68	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(replace(h, alpha=0.05)) != canonical_hypothesis_digest(
69	        replace(h, alpha=0.10)
70	    )
71	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(h) != canonical_hypothesis_digest(
72	        replace(h, stability_threshold=0.9)
73	    )
74	
75	
76	def test_digest_is_stable_and_does_not_change_when_locked():
77	    # locking adds locked_at/timestamp_proof, which are EXCLUDED from the digest — so the digest
78	    # the runner recomputes on the locked hypothesis equals the one the proof was issued over.
79	    h = _unlocked()
80	    d_before = canonical_hypothesis_digest(h)
81	    locked = lock_hypothesis(h, authority=_auth(), instant=_past())
82	    assert canonical_hypothesis_digest(locked) == d_before
83	
84	
85	# ---- lock_hypothesis + verify round-trip ----
86	
87	
88	def test_lock_then_verify_roundtrip():
89	    auth = _auth()
90	    locked = lock_hypothesis(_unlocked(), authority=auth, instant=_past())
91	    assert locked.locked
92	    vt = verify_preregistration(locked, authority=auth)
93	    assert vt.authority == "local-hmac"
94	    assert vt.digest == canonical_hypothesis_digest(locked)
95	    assert vt.instant.tzinfo is not None
96	
97	
98	def test_relocking_is_forbidden():
99	    auth = _auth()
100	    locked = lock_hypothesis(_unlocked(), authority=auth, instant=_past())
101	    with pytest.raises(PreRegistrationError, match="already locked"):
102	        lock_hypothesis(locked, authority=auth, instant=_past())
103	
104	
105	# ---- the breaks pre-registration must catch ----
106	
107	
108	def test_unlocked_hypothesis_is_rejected():
109	    with pytest.raises(PreRegistrationError, match="not locked"):
110	        verify_preregistration(_unlocked(), authority=_auth())

content.text:
Files are restored (the system reminders show the originals are back in place). Full suites pass: 63 tests.

My verification is complete.

Findings:
- confirm.py `_resolve_locked_param` (lines 270-285) cross-checks confirm-time alpha/stability against locked values; mismatch raises, locked value wins, None falls through to supplied. Wired into both `confirm_unit_level` (alpha, line 207) and `confirm_whole_corpus` (alpha + stability_threshold, lines 362-368).
- hypothesis.py adds optional `alpha`/`stability_threshold` fields with bounds validation in `__post_init__` (lines 79-98).
- preregistration.py binds both thresholds into the digest (lines 118-122), so a post-lock change invalidates the proof.
- All three named tests pass and genuinely discriminate: reverting the cross-check made both confirm tests fail; removing the digest binding made the digest test fail.
- No regression: full test_confirm.py + test_preregistration.py (63 tests) pass.

Minor note (not a defect): `test_locked_stability_threshold_cannot_be_overridden_at_confirm_time` uses `object.__setattr__` to simulate the locked field rather than constructing it directly, but it still exercises the real `_resolve_locked_param` path and fails on revert, so it discriminates.

input.reasoning:
Appropriate: hypothesis.py adds optional alpha/stability_threshold fields with bounds validation in __post_init__ (in (0,0.5) and (0,1]); preregistration.py binds both into canonical_hypothesis_digest (lines 118-122) so a post-lock change breaks the proof; confirm.py adds _resolve_locked_param (lines 270-285) which raises on a confirm-time value contradicting the locked one, lets None fall through to supplied (exploratory), and is wired into confirm_unit_level (alpha) and confirm_whole_corpus (alpha + stability_threshold). The locked value wins. Idiomatic, mirrors the existing predicted_direction lock pattern (#G-001), no regression — full test_confirm.py + test_preregistration.py (63 tests) pass.

Complete/discriminating: I reverted the fix (neutered _resolve_locked_param to return supplied, and removed alpha/stability_threshold from the digest payload) and re-ran the three named tests — all three FAILED (the two confirm tests no longer raised on a mismatched threshold; the digest test saw identical hashes for differing alpha). Restoring the originals returned all to green. The tests genuinely fail if the fix is reverted. (Minor, non-blocking: the stability test uses object.__setattr__ to seed the locked field rather than constructing it, but it still exercises the real cross-check path and discriminates.)

content.text:
Verification complete. Verdict: CONFIRMED.

Fix H-001 is both appropriate and complete. The implementation correctly closes the threshold-HARKing path by making alpha and stability_threshold optional digest-bound hypothesis fields, with confirmers cross-checking confirm-time arguments against locked values (mismatch raises). All three named tests pass and genuinely discriminate — I confirmed each one fails when the fix is reverted. No regressions across the 63 tests in the two affected suites.
